$comment: Compat scoreboard fixtures — popular OSS workflows we test rh against on every PR + nightly. Add new entries when a real-world repro of a compat gap surfaces. Each row pins (repo, ref, workflow path, expected outcome) so a regression is unambiguous: a PASS that turns into a FAIL is a real compat break, not flakiness.

fixtures.name: typey
fixtures.rationale: Canonical first-external-repo validation target. 9-cell os×bun matrix; we filter to ubuntu since cross-OS needs Docker.

fixtures.name: kleur
fixtures.rationale: v0.6.17 worktree ELOOP regression target. Workflow uses c8 coverage which walks the source tree; pre-fix this hit Windows ELOOP. We don't require pass — workflow has unrelated legacy-Node-version failures — but ZERO `ELOOP` mentions in the log.

fixtures.name: workflow-dispatch-input
fixtures.rationale: v0.6.16 workflow_dispatch + --input/-i target. Synthetic workflow asserts via bash `test` that the sentinel string round-trips through ${{ inputs.sentinel }}. Replaces the original tj-actions/changed-files fixture which used a composite-action `with:` and never echoed the input to stdout, making the assertion unreliable.

fixtures.name: is-plain-obj
fixtures.rationale: Tiny package (~10 lines of code), fast install. Smoke test for the host-shim path (actions/checkout, setup-node, no real action download).
